part1_assertions.label: no planned source files on disk

files_written.description: This file contains a single function `add(a, b)` which takes two parameters and returns their sum. It is exported using `module.exports` for use in other files.

This file serves as the entry point of the program. It imports the `add` function from `add.js`, calls `add(2, 3)`, and prints the result to the console, ensuring only the integer result is outputted, as per the acceptance criteria.

builder_summary: Planned and organized the implementation of a simple Node.js project with two files: add.js for exporting an addition function and run.js for invoking the function and printing the result. The use of CommonJS was chosen for broader Node.js compatibility.
